hypothesis: Introducing aromatic substitutions at position 5 and hydrophobic substitutions at position 11 will enhance SSTR2-specific interactions with ECL2/ECL3 and TM5/TM6, while avoiding off-target compatibility with SSTR3/5. This will increase the selectivity margin without compromising SSTR2 binding affinity.

critic_feedback_used.hypothesis: The current designs achieve strong SSTR2 binding but lack sufficient selectivity due to conserved off-target interactions. Targeting non-pharmacophore positions for mutagenesis and tightening the ddG threshold will improve selectivity while preserving binding affinity.

proposed_changes.rationale: To filter out candidates with weaker binding (higher ddG) and retain only the most stable designs, which may improve selectivity by reducing off-target interactions.
To target non-pharmacophore positions where mutations can disrupt off-target contacts (especially SSTR3/SSTR5) without affecting the conserved FWKT pharmacophore or Cys3/Cys14.
